steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$g(-7) = -28 - 6 = -34.$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. It's given that the function $g$ is defined by $g\left(x\right)=4x-6$. Substituting $-7$ for $x$ into the given equation yields $g\left(-7\right)=4\left(-7\right)-6$, or $g\left(-7\right)=-34$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $x$ for which $g\left(x\right)=-7$, not the value of $g\left(-7\right)$.
